title: Iron in Fluid
body: Archimedes' principle can be used to calculate the density of a fluid as well as that of a solid. Suppose a chunk of iron with a mass of $$390.0$$ g in air is found to have an apparent mass of $$350.5$$ g when completely submerged in an unknown liquid. The density of iron (Table $$14.1)$$ is $$7.86$$ $$\frac{g}{{cm}^3}$$.
